option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Azure Deployment Plan for PhotoAlbum-Java Project ## **Goal** Deploy the PhotoAlbum-Java application to Azure using Azure Developer CLI (AZD) with Azure Container Apps and Azure Database for PostgreSQL. ## **Project Information** **PhotoAlbum-Java** - **Stack**: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.4.0 and Maven - **Type**: Photo gallery web application with drag-and-drop upload functionality - **Database**: PostgreSQL 15 for photo metadata and BLOB storage - **Containerization**: Dockerfile present (multi-stage build with Maven and OpenJDK 21) - **Dependencies**: PostgreSQL database - **Hosting**: Azure Container Apps ## **Azure Resources Architecture** > **Install the mermaid extension in IDE to view the architecture.** ```mermaid graph TD %% Services svcazurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp["Name: photoalbum-java-app
Path: C:/Users/yueli6/Desktop/CodeToCloud/PhotoAlbum-Java
Language: java"] subgraph "Compute Resources" %% Resources subgraph containerappenv["Azure Container Apps (ACA) Environment"] azurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp("photoalbum-java-app (Azure Container App)") end containerappenv:::cluster end subgraph "Dependency Resources" %% Dependency Resources azure_postgres["Azure Database for PostgreSQL"] end subgraph "Supporting Services" acr["Azure Container Registry"] appinsights["Application Insights"] loganalytics["Log Analytics Workspace"] keyvault["Azure Key Vault"] managedidentity["User-Assigned Managed Identity"] end %% Relationships svcazurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp --> |"hosted on"| azurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp azurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp -.-> |"depends on"| azure_postgres azurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp --> |"pulls image from"| acr azurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp --> |"logs to"| appinsights azurecontainerapps_photoalbumjavaapp --> |"uses identity"| managedidentity managedidentity --> |"pulls secrets from"| keyvault keyvault -.-> |"stores connection string"| azure_postgres appinsights --> |"sends logs to"| loganalytics ``` **Resource Relationships:** - The container app pulls its Docker image from Azure Container Registry - The app connects to Azure Database for PostgreSQL for photo metadata and BLOB storage - User-Assigned Managed Identity is used for secure access to Key Vault and Container Registry - Database connection string is stored securely in Azure Key Vault - Application logs are sent to Application Insights and Log Analytics Workspace ## **Recommended Azure Resources** ### Application: photoalbum-java-app - **Hosting Service Type**: Azure Container Apps - **SKU**: Consumption-based (0.5 vCPU, 1 GB memory) - Auto-scales from 0-10 replicas based on HTTP traffic - Suitable for web applications with variable load - **Configuration**: - Language: Java 21 - Port: 8080 - Environment Variables: - `SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE`: "production" - `POSTGRES_HOST`: Reference to PostgreSQL server endpoint - `POSTGRES_DATABASE`: "postgres" - `POSTGRES_USERNAME`: Reference from Key Vault - `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`: Reference from Key Vault - dockerFilePath: `C:/Users/yueli6/Desktop/CodeToCloud/PhotoAlbum-Java/Dockerfile` - dockerContext: `C:/Users/yueli6/Desktop/CodeToCloud/PhotoAlbum-Java` ### Dependency: Azure Database for PostgreSQL - **Dependency Name**: postgres - **SKU**: Burstable B1ms (1 vCore, 2 GB memory, 32 GB storage) - Suitable for development and light production workloads - Cost-effective for moderate traffic applications - **Service Type**: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server - **Connection Type**: Connection string stored in Key Vault, accessed via Managed Identity - **Configuration**: - PostgreSQL Version: 15 - High Availability: Disabled (can be enabled for production) - Backup Retention: 7 days - **Environment Variables**: - `POSTGRES_HOST`: PostgreSQL server FQDN - `POSTGRES_DATABASE`: "postgres" - `POSTGRES_USERNAME`: "photoalbum" - `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`: Secure password (stored in Key Vault) ## **Recommended Supporting Services** 1. **Azure Container Registry** - SKU: Basic (sufficient for development/testing) - Purpose: Store and manage Docker container images - Admin access: Disabled (use Managed Identity with AcrPull role) 2. **Application Insights** - Purpose: Application performance monitoring and logging - Connected to Log Analytics Workspace 3. **Log Analytics Workspace** - Purpose: Centralized logging for all Azure resources - Retention: 30 days 4. **Azure Key Vault** - SKU: Standard - Purpose: Securely store PostgreSQL connection string and credentials - Access: Via User-Assigned Managed Identity 5. **User-Assigned Managed Identity** - Purpose: Secure authentication for Container App to access: - Azure Container Registry (AcrPull role) - Azure Key Vault (secrets access) ## **Recommended Security Configurations** 1. **Managed Identity Permissions**: - User-Assigned Managed Identity must have `AcrPull` role (7f951dda-4ed3-4680-a7ca-43fe172d538d) on Container Registry - User-Assigned Managed Identity must have `Key Vault Secrets User` role on Key Vault - User-Assigned Managed Identity must be assigned to the Container App 2. **Key Vault Access**: - Store PostgreSQL connection string as secret - Container App references secrets via Key Vault URI 3. **Network Security**: - PostgreSQL firewall rules to allow Azure services - Container App uses managed identity (no hardcoded credentials) 4. **Database Security**: - SSL/TLS enforcement enabled on PostgreSQL - Strong password policy - Regular automated backups ## **Execution Steps** > **Below are the steps for Copilot to follow; ask Copilot to update or execute this plan.** ### 1. Containerization - [x] Check existing Dockerfile: `C:/Users/yueli6/Desktop/CodeToCloud/PhotoAlbum-Java/Dockerfile` - [ ] Verify Dockerfile is compatible with Azure Container Apps - [ ] Test Docker build locally (optional) - **Output**: Docker artifacts ready for deployment ### 2. Create Azure Infrastructure Files for AZD - [ ] Get current Azure subscription ID - [ ] Call `appmod-get-available-region-sku` to get available regions and SKUs for: - Microsoft.App/containerApps - Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL/flexibleServers - Microsoft.ContainerRegistry/registries - Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults - Microsoft.Insights/components - [ ] Call `appmod-get-iac-rules` to get IaC generation rules for AZD with Bicep - [ ] Generate infrastructure files: - `azure.yaml` - AZD project configuration - `infra/main.bicep` - Main infrastructure template - `infra/main.parameters.json` - Parameters file - `infra/resources.bicep` - Resource definitions - `infra/app/` - Container app specific configurations - [ ] Call `get_errors` on generated Bicep files - [ ] Fix any errors and validate - **Output**: `azure.yaml`, `infra/main.bicep`, `infra/main.parameters.json`, and supporting files ### 3. Environment Setup for AZD - [ ] Check if Azure CLI (az) is installed - [ ] Check if Azure Developer CLI (azd) is installed - [ ] Run `azd env new photoalbum-java-env --no-prompt` to create environment - [ ] Set required environment variables: - `azd env set AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID ` - `azd env set AZURE_LOCATION ` (from available regions) - `azd env set POSTGRES_PASSWORD ` - [ ] Create resource group with `az group create --name --location ` - [ ] Set `azd env set AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP ` - **Output**: Environment configured and ready for provisioning ### 4. Deployment - [ ] Run `azd provision --preview --no-prompt` to preview infrastructure - [ ] Review and confirm resources to be created - [ ] Run `azd up --no-prompt` to provision and deploy: - Provision all Azure resources - Build Docker image - Push to Azure Container Registry - Deploy to Azure Container Apps - [ ] Monitor deployment progress - [ ] If deployment fails, analyze errors and retry - [ ] If region issues occur, run `azd down --force --no-prompt` and select new region - **Output**: Application deployed and running on Azure ### 5. Deployment Validation - [ ] Call `appmod-get-azd-app-logs` to retrieve application logs - [ ] Verify application is running without errors - [ ] Test application endpoint (HTTP GET to home page) - [ ] Verify database connectivity - [ ] Test photo upload functionality - **Output**: Validated working application ### 6. Summarize Deployment Result - [ ] Call `appmod-summarize-result` tool to generate deployment summary - [ ] Document all provisioned resources - [ ] Document application endpoint URLs - [ ] Generate architecture diagram of deployed resources - **Output**: `.azure/summary.copilotmd` with deployment details ## **Progress Tracking** Progress will be tracked in `.azure/progress.copilotmd` after each step: **Current Status**: Plan created, ready to begin execution - [x] Deployment plan created - [ ] Containerization verified - [ ] Azure infrastructure files generated - [ ] AZD environment configured - [ ] Resources provisioned - [ ] Application deployed - [ ] Deployment validated - [ ] Summary generated --- **Next Action**: Begin execution with Step 1 (Containerization verification)

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Deployment Progress Tracking
## Current Status: In Progress
### Step 1: Containerization ✅ COMPLETED - [x] Dockerfile exists at `C:/Users/yueli6/Desktop/CodeToCloud/PhotoAlbum-Java/Dockerfile` - [x] Dockerfile verified - Multi-stage build with Maven and OpenJDK 21 - [x] Port 8080 configured correctly - **Status**: Ready for Azure deployment
### Step 2: Create Azure Infrastructure Files for AZD IN PROGRESS - [ ] Get current Azure subscription ID - [ ] Check available regions and SKUs - [ ] Get IaC generation rules - [ ] Generate azure.yaml - [ ] Generate infra/main.bicep - [ ] Generate infra/main.parameters.json - [ ] Validate Bicep files - **Status**: Starting...
### Step 3: Environment Setup for AZD ⏳ PENDING - [ ] Verify Azure CLI installed - [ ] Verify Azure Developer CLI installed - [ ] Create azd environment - [ ] Configure environment variables - [ ] Create resource group - **Status**: Not started
### Step 4: Deployment ⏳ PENDING - [ ] Preview infrastructure (azd provision --preview) - [ ] Deploy application (azd up) - [ ] Monitor deployment - **Status**: Not started
### Step 5: Deployment Validation ⏳ PENDING - [ ] Retrieve application logs - [ ] Test application endpoint - [ ] Verify database connectivity - **Status**: Not started
### Step 6: Summary ⏳ PENDING - [ ] Generate deployment summary - **Status**: Not started
--- **Last Updated**: Step 1 completed - Dockerfile verified **Next Action**: Get Azure subscription and check available regions

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/refs/heads/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json
name: photoalbum-java metadata: template: photoalbum-java@0.0.1
services: photoalbum-java-app: project: . language: java host: containerapp docker: path: ./Dockerfile context: .

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
# Spring Boot Production Configuration server.port=8080
# Character encoding server.servlet.encoding.charset=UTF-8 server.servlet.encoding.enabled=true server.servlet.encoding.force=true
# PostgreSQL Database Configuration (will be overridden by environment variables) spring.datasource.url=${SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL} spring.datasource.username=${SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME} spring.datasource.password=${SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD} spring.datasource.driver-class-name=org.postgresql.Driver
# JPA Configuration spring.jpa.database-platform=org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update spring.jpa.show-sql=false spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.format_sql=false
# File Upload Configuration spring.servlet.multipart.max-file-size=10MB spring.servlet.multipart.max-request-size=50MB
# Application Configuration - File Upload Validation app.file-upload.max-file-size-bytes=10485760 app.file-upload.allowed-mime-types=image/jpeg,image/png,image/gif,image/webp app.file-upload.max-files-per-upload=10
# Logging logging.level.com.photoalbum=INFO logging.level.org.springframework.web=INFO
# Application Insights azure.application-insights.instrumentation-key=${APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING:}
